model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Arkansas is a strong 4-seed ranked 17th in KenPom with an adjusted efficiency margin of +25.43, nearly 20 points higher than Hawai'i's +5.59, driven by the nation's 5th-ranked offense (AdjO 127.6) that should overwhelm Hawai'i's below-average attack (AdjO 107.0, 211th nationally). While Hawai'i boasts a surprisingly solid defense ranked 52nd nationally, their anemic offense built against Big West competition will struggle to generate enough scoring to keep pace with Arkansas's SEC-tested firepower. The roughly 20-point efficiency gap is significantly wider than the typical 4/13 matchup, making this one of the safer 4-seed picks in the bracket despite the historically upset-prone nature of this seed line.

reasoningStep.title: Arkansas vs Hawai'i – West 4/13 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arkansas's elite offense (5th nationally) and massive 20-point efficiency margin advantage over Hawai'i make this a comfortable pick for the Razorbacks despite Hawai'i's solid defensive profile.
evidence: Arkansas KenPom #17: AdjEM +25.43, AdjO 127.6 (5th), AdjD 102.1; Torvik #20 with 25-8 SEC record

modelTrace.content:
Search is down, but I have strong ratings data for both teams. Let me compile my analysis.

**Arkansas (4-seed, SEC)**
- KenPom: #17, AdjEM +25.43, AdjO 127.6 (5th), AdjD 102.1
- Torvik: #20, AdjOE 127.8 (5th), AdjDE 102.0 (50th)
- Record: 25-8
- Conference: SEC

**Hawai'i (13-seed, Big West)**
- KenPom: #110, AdjEM +5.59, AdjO 107.0 (211th), AdjD 101.5
- Torvik: #118, AdjOE 106.8 (208th), AdjDE 102.1 (52nd)
- Record: 23-8
- Conference: Big West

The gap is enormous. Arkansas has a ~20-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage. Arkansas's elite offense (5th nationally) vs Hawai'i's below-average offense (208th-211th) is the key differentiator. Interestingly, Hawai'i has a solid defense (52nd in both systems), which could keep this from being a total blowout, but their offense is far too weak to keep pace with Arkansas's firepower. Historically, 4-seeds beat 13-seeds about 79% of the time, and this matchup features a wider quality gap than typical 4/13 games.
